text.title: Reporting Dashboards Examples
text.summary: End-to-end worked example illustrating the pipeline against a realistic sprint.

example.title: Weekly sprint Slack digest — happy path
example.note: Sprint S-2026-W21. Audit: 0 issues without estimate, 0 stale Done; OK. Schema loaded (on_track_min=0.85). Fetch (read-only token, 24 issues paginated). Compute: completed=22/24=0.917, scope_creep=2 added Day 3, blocked_count=0. Threshold: completion>=0.85 + scope_creep<=3 + blocked==0 → status="On Track". Validate: committed=24, fetch_errors=0, sprint_start_date=2026-05-19 → pass. Archive: s3://reports/sprint-2026-w21.md. Deliver: Slack #leadership with archive link. delivered_to=["#leadership"]. Lesson: validation gate is cheap; multi-metric prevented "On Track" mislabel had scope_creep been 12.

example.title: 0% report shipped — what the pipeline must catch
example.note: Sprint S-2026-W21. Audit: skipped (operator bypassed step 1). Fetch: Jira returned 401 (token rotated overnight) → empty array. Compute: completed=0/0; division-safe returned 0. THE PIPELINE THAT WAS ACTUALLY SHIPPED then delivered "0% completion — Off Track" to #leadership. CORRECT pipeline blocks at step 5: committed=0 → emit error alert + exit non-zero; nothing posted. Repair: keep read-only token rotation observable; emit alert; never deliver degraded report.
